mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate environments are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal triggers but SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in >70% of cases, or unemployment spikes fail to precede negative earnings revisions within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, boosting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with negative SPX 60-day returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing and supply chain throughput, which immediately improves short-term earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict 5-day returns during tightening periods, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 and no significant alpha generation.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels combined with restrictive federal funds rates create a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint high-inflation-high-rate state yields positive SPX returns in >65% of observations, or the interaction effect reverses sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports aggregate demand, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day SPX returns, or the signal's predictive power decays to Rank IC < 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent annualized signals severe demand contraction and recession risk, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDP growth below threshold fails to precede negative market moves, with positive returns occurring in >70% of triggered windows over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized increases in industrial production and retail sales confirm broad-based economic momentum without inventory bottlenecks, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series baseline models.
